steps.text: The triangle has three sides: one with length $x$ and two with length $y$ each.
Perimeter = sum of all sides: $x + y + y = x + 2y$. Setting equal to 47: $x + 2y = 47$.

• A: $x + y = 47$ — accounts for only two sides, not three. Eliminated.
  • B: $x + 2y = 47$ — correctly counts one $x$-side and two $y$-sides. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • C: $2x + y = 47$ — would require two sides of length $x$ and one of length $y$, opposite of the given. Eliminated.
  • D: $2x + 2y = 47$ — four sides total; a triangle has only three. Eliminated.
